1.title: A shepherd, not a courtier
1.text: Amos was 'among the herdmen of Tekoa' — a working man from the Judean hills, not a court prophet. His words are dated 'two years before the earthquake,' a quake still remembered generations later.

1.template: But I will send a fire into the house of {0}

1.question: In Amos 1:9, why is judgment pronounced against Tyre?
options: They delivered up the whole captivity to Edom, and remembered not the brotherly covenant
They burned the king of Edom's bones

1.question: In Amos 1:13, what is charged against the children of Ammon?
options: They ripped up the women with child of Gilead, to enlarge their border
They made a treaty with Egypt

2.question: In Amos 2:4, why is judgment pronounced against Judah?
options: They burned the bones of the king of Edom
They despised the law of the Lord and have not kept his commandments
They oppressed the kine of Bashan

2.template: they sold the righteous for silver, and the poor for a pair of {0}

2.title: Tall as the cedars
2.text: Verse 9 recalls how the Lord destroyed the Amorite before Israel — 'whose height was like the height of the cedars, and he was strong as the oaks' — a reminder that Israel's land was a gift, not an achievement.

2.question: According to Amos 2:11, whom did the Lord raise up from among Israel's sons and young men?

3.question: In Amos 3:3, what rhetorical question opens the chain of images?
options: Can two walk together, except they be agreed?
Can a fish live on dry land?
Will the sun rise at midnight?
Can a fig tree bear olives?

3.title: God reveals his secret
3.text: Amos 3:7 — 'the Lord God will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets' — is one of the Bible's clearest statements that God works through prophets.

3.template: For they know not to do {0}

3.question: According to Amos 3:15, which houses will the Lord smite?
options: The winter house with the summer house, and the houses of ivory

4.template: Hear this word, ye {0} of Bashan

4.question: In Amos 4:4, to which two places does the Lord ironically say 'come and transgress'?
What refrain closes each of the Lord's chastisements in Amos 4?
options: I will have mercy on you
Yet have ye not returned unto me
Return, and I will heal you

4.question: How does Amos 4:13 describe the God of hosts?
options: He that formeth the mountains, and createth the wind, and declareth unto man what is his thought

5.question: In Amos 5:4, what does the Lord tell the house of Israel?
options: Seek ye me, and ye shall live

5.title: The maker of the constellations
5.text: Amos 5:8 names the Lord as the one who 'maketh the seven stars and Orion' — pointing Israel from their idols to the God who set the very constellations in the sky.

5.question: In Amos 5:8, what does the Lord do with the waters of the sea?
options: He calls for them and pours them out upon the face of the earth

5.template: They hate him that rebuketh in the {0}

5.question: According to Amos 5:14, what are the people to seek that they may live?

5.template: Hate the evil, and love the good, and establish {0} in the gate

5.question: According to Amos 5:18-20, what will the day of the Lord be for those who desire it?
In Amos 5:24, what does the Lord say should run down as waters?

6.title: Beds of ivory
6.text: Verse 4 pictures the elite lying 'upon beds of ivory' and eating lambs and calves — archaeology at Samaria has uncovered ivory inlays that match this indictment of luxury.

6.question: In Amos 6:6, why is woe pronounced on those who drink wine in bowls?
options: They are not grieved for the affliction of Joseph
They forgot to keep the Sabbath

6.template: for ye have turned judgment into {0}

6.question: According to Amos 6:14, what will the Lord raise up against the house of Israel?
options: A nation to afflict them from the entering in of Hemath

7.title: The prophet who argued back
7.text: In chapter 7 Amos does not merely deliver judgment — he intercedes. Twice he pleads 'O Lord God, forgive... by whom shall Jacob arise? for he is small,' and twice the Lord relents.

7.question: In the first two visions of Amos 7, what does Amos plead about Jacob?
options: By whom shall Jacob arise? for he is small

7.question: In the third vision, what does the Lord hold in his hand upon the wall (Amos 7:7)?

7.template: I was no prophet, neither was I a prophet’s son; but I was an {0}

7.question: How does the priest Amaziah try to silence Amos (Amos 7:12-13)?
options: By telling him to flee to Judah and prophesy not at Beth-el, the king's chapel

8.template: and behold a {0} of summer fruit

8.question: In Amos 8:5, how do the greedy merchants cheat the poor?
options: Making the ephah small, and the shekel great, and falsifying the balances

8.question: According to Amos 8:10, what will the Lord turn the people's feasts into?
In Amos 8:11, what kind of famine does the Lord promise to send?
options: A famine of bread and water
A famine of hearing the words of the Lord

9.question: In Amos 9:2-3, what does the Lord say about escaping his judgment?
options: No hiding place — not hell, heaven, Carmel, nor the bottom of the sea — will deliver them

9.title: No place to hide
9.text: The fifth and final vision opens with the Lord standing upon the altar. Its poetry — 'though they climb up to heaven, thence will I bring them down' — echoes the reach later sung in Psalm 139.

9.question: According to Amos 9:8, what does the Lord promise about the house of Jacob?
options: He will not utterly destroy it

9.template: will I raise up the tabernacle of {0} that is fallen

9.question: How does the book of Amos close, in Amos 9:14-15?
options: With a promise to bring again the captivity of Israel and plant them upon their land
